note: IB Diploma Programme Group 3 — Individuals and Societies. Subjects include History, Economics, Geography, Psychology, Business Management, Global Politics, and more. All at SL and HL.

topics.subject: History
topics.description: Analysis of primary and secondary historical sources on 5 prescribed subjects (e.g. Military Leaders, Causes and Effects of 20th century wars, Rights and Protest). Skills: provenance, reliability, bias, corroboration.

topics.subject: History
topics.subtopicTitle: Comparative essay across two world history topics
topics.description: 12 world history topics including: Causes and effects of 20th century wars; Authoritarian states; The Cold War; Decolonisation; Rights and protest. Two topics studied; essay comparing events from different regions.

topics.subject: History
topics.subtopicTitle: Depth study of a world region
topics.description: Four regional options (Europe, Americas, Asia and Oceania, Africa and Middle East). Three in-depth sections studied; three essay questions answered in exam. Requires detailed knowledge of causes, developments, and significance.

topics.subject: History
topics.description: Student-chosen historical question; research using primary and secondary sources; evaluation of sources; analysis and argument; reflection on the methods of historians.

topics.subject: Economics
topics.description: Scarcity and opportunity cost; PPF; demand and supply analysis; market equilibrium; price elasticity; cross-elasticity; income elasticity; HL: price discrimination, monopoly, oligopoly

topics.subject: Economics
topics.description: Externalities; public goods; information asymmetry; government policies (taxes, subsidies, price controls); evaluation of intervention; common pool resources; HL: market power and regulation

topics.subject: Economics
topics.subtopicTitle: GDP, unemployment, inflation, and macroeconomic policy
topics.description: National income accounting; AD/AS model; types of unemployment; causes and effects of inflation; fiscal and monetary policy; supply-side policies; HL: Keynesian vs monetarist debate

topics.subject: Economics
topics.subtopicTitle: Trade, exchange rates, and balance of payments
topics.description: Comparative advantage; trade barriers and free trade agreements; exchange rate systems; balance of payments; current account deficits; HL: Marshall-Lerner condition; J-curve

topics.subject: Economics
topics.description: Definitions and measures of development (GDP, HDI, MPI); economic and social indicators; barriers to development; strategies (trade, aid, microfinance, debt relief); sustainability
